data.title: Rio Tinto selected for second major Chile lithium project
data.description:
Rio Tinto (NYSE:RIO) said Thursday it was selected by Chile's government for the second time this week for a major new lithium project, with the state-run mining company Empresa Nacional de Minería naming it a partner for its Altoandinos lithium project.

Rio (NYSE:RIO), which would acquire an initial 51% stake in the project while ENAMI holds the remaining 49%, will provide an estimated $425M including a pre-feasibility study, use of its pilot plant at the Rincon project in Argentina, and the use of its direct lithium extraction technology; ENAMI said the project will represent a total investment of $3B.

Earlier this week, Rio (RIO) also was selected by state-run copper miner Codelco for the Maricunga lithium project, agreeing to contribute up to $900M in the deal.

With a strategic pipeline of tier 1 lithium assets and options, including those from the recent acquisition of Arcadium Lithium, Rio Tinto (RIO) now holds one of the world's largest lithium portfolios.

data.title: LPL Financial advisory and brokerage assets inch down 0.4% M/M in April
data.description:
LPL Financial's (NASDAQ:LPLA) total advisory and brokerage assets totaled $1.79T at the end of April, down 0.4% from the end of March, it said on Thursday. That's still up 27% from a year ago.

Total organic net new assets for April were $6.1B, translating to a 4.1% annualized growth rate. 

Prior to the impacts of $0.2B of assets that off-boarded in April as part of the previously disclosed planned separation from misaligned large OSJs, alongside $0.1B of assets from Wintrust Investments, LLC and certain private client business at Great Lakes Advisors, LLC, organic net new assets were $6.2B, translating to 4.1% annualized growth.

That's well below the $13.1B recorded in March and $7.0B logged a year earlier.

Total acquired net new assets were $0 in April, compared with $7.1B in March and $5.0B in April 2024.

Total clients cash balances fell 2.4% sequentially and climbed 13.3% Y/Y to $51.8B.

data.title: Deckers Outdoor tops FQ1 estimates and adds buyback firepower, but profit guidance weighs on shares
data.description:
Deckers Outdoor Corporation (NYSE:DECK) slid in postmarket trading on Thursday after reporting sales rose 6.5% in FQ4 to $1.03 billion. On a constant currency basis, sales increased by 7.5% during the quarter.

Domestic net sales were flat during the quarter at $611.6 million, and international sales increased 19.9% to $374.1 million. Sales from the direct-to-consumer channel decreased 1.2% to $410.2 million.

Brand sales growth: UGG +3.6% to $374.3 million, HOKA +10.0% to $586.1 million, Other brands -6.3% to $61.3 million.

The California-based company said gross margin rose to 56.7% of sales from 56.3% a year ago. Operating income was $173.9 million, compared to $144.3 million a year ago. EPS was reported at $1.00 vs. $0.61 consensus and $0.82 a year ago.

CEO update: "While the global trade environment has introduced greater near-term uncertainty, we are very confident in the exciting opportunities ahead for HOKA and UGG. We view these brands as industry leaders, each with iconic and innovative products that operate in differentiated marketplaces. Alongside Deckers’ superb balance sheet, this positions us well to manage through the near-term with a focus on the long-term."

During FQ4, the company repurchased approximately 1.78 million shares of its common stock for a total of $266.0 million at a weighted average price paid per share of $149.62. Deckers (NYSE:DECK) ended the quarter with an inventory position of $495.2 million, vs. $474.3 million a year ago.

Looking ahead, Deckers (DECK) guided for FQ1 sales to be in a range of $890 million to $910 million and EPS of $0.62 to $0.67  (midpoint $0.645) vs. $0.79 consensus. On the capital allocation front, the board approved an increase of $2.25 billion to the company's stock repurchase authorization, which brings its total outstanding authorization for buybacks to approximately $2.5 billion.

Deckers Brands (DECK) also announced the appointment of Cindy Davis as Chair of the Board of Directors, effective immediately. She succeeds Mike Devine, III, who is retiring from the board after over 14 years of service, including six years as Chair. Davis joined the Board in 2018 and has served as Chair of its Talent & Compensation Committee since 2019 and as a member of its Corporate Responsibility, Sustainability & Governance Committee since 2021. 

Shares of Deckers Outdoor (DECK) fell 10.9% in the postmarket session on Thursday, after adding 2.8% during the regular session. The footwear stock is down almost 45% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Cavco Non-GAAP EPS of $5.40 beats by $0.60, revenue of $508.36M beats by $4.21M
data.description:
• Cavco press release (NASDAQ:CVCO): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $5.40 beats by $0.60.

data.title: Safran sees Boeing regaining momentum on 737 Max production
data.description:
Boeing (NYSE:BA) is showing signs of renewed production momentum on its 737 Max program following years of turbulence, Olivier Andries, chief executive of French aerospace group Safran (OTCPK:SAFRY) (OTCPK:SAFRF), a major supplier to the U.S. planemaker, said Thursday, according to a report by Reuters.

Speaking at Safran’s annual shareholder meeting, Andries said Boeing’s (NYSE:BA) production pace on the single-aisle 737 Max has reached nearly 38 jets a month, the current maximum allowed by U.S. aviation regulators following a mid-air panel failure on an Alaska Airlines flight last year.

Boeing Commercial Airplanes’ vice president of quality, Doug Ackerman, also said earlier this week that the company aims to stabilize monthly output at 38 aircraft over the coming months.

Safran (OTCPK:SAFRY) (OTCPK:SAFRF), which jointly builds the CFM International LEAP engine with GE Aerospace (NYSE:GE), supplies powerplants for all Boeing (BA) 737 Max aircraft. The LEAP also competes with Pratt & Whitney on the Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) A320neo platform.

The Safran chief also noted that the company is benefiting from increased demand for aftermarket engine services, a trend he attributed partly to ongoing delays in new aircraft deliveries amid ongoing supply chain challenges across the aerospace industry.

data.title: Codere Online receives Nasdaq delisting notice
data.description:
• Codere Online (NASDAQ:CDRO) received a Nasdaq delisting notice on May 16, 2025, due to its failure to file its 2024 Form 20-F on time.
  • The filing delay stems from the prolonged audit process following the appointment of a new independent auditor on December 31, 2024.
  • Codere filed its delayed 2023 Form 20-F on May 1, 2025, and is working to file the 2024 report by May 30, 2025.
  • CDRO has formally requested a hearing to appeal the delisting and a further stay of trading suspension during the review.
  • If Nasdaq denies the trading stay, Codere’s securities will be suspended on June 6, 2025.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Magyar Bancorp announces buyback program for up to 5% of outstanding shares
data.description:
Magyar Bancorp (NASDAQ:MGYR) announced on Thursday a stock repurchase program to buy back up to 5% of the community bank's outstanding shares.

The total number of shares set to be repurchased is expected to be up to 323,547.

The company said the repurchased shares will be held as treasury stock and will be available for general corporate purposes.

Additionally, Magyar said it has completed its previously announced buyback program, having repurchased 337,146 shares at an average price of $12.23.

data.title: Workday Non-GAAP EPS of $2.23 beats by $0.22, revenue of $2.24B beats by $20M

    • Non-GAAP operating margin of 28.0%1

    Workday is updating guidance for the fiscal 2026 full year ending January 31, 2026, as follows:

    • Non-GAAP operating margin of 28.5%1
